title: APC Smart-UPS Battery Runtime Remaining Below Safe Threshold
dataSources: APC PowerNet-MIB OID `1.3.6.1.4.1.318.1.1.1.2.2.3` (`upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining`, vendor-reported minutes remaining at present load) polled via `Splunk_TA_snmp` (Splunkbase #1537) and Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) into `index=power` `sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups`. Auto-extracted fields: `upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining`, `upsAdvOutputLoad`, `upsAdvBatteryCapacity`, `upsAdvBatteryReplaceIndicator`, `host`. Calculated field `runtime_min = tonumber(upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining)/6000` at the search head.
app: Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266), SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537)
spl:
index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host=*
| eval runtime_min=tonumber(upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining)/6000
| timechart span=1h avg(runtime_min) as avg_runtime by host
| where avg_runtime < 15
description: Detects APC Smart-UPS units whose 1-hour rolling-average runtime estimate (`upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining`) drops below 15 minutes — the operational guardrail that signals weak batteries, growing load, or unstable input power before an outage causes an ungraceful shutdown of the connected servers and SAN.
value: Forces battery replacement or load-shedding decisions before predicted runtime collapses, preventing brownout-driven SAN failovers and emergency-shutdown filesystem corruption that take days to recover from after a real outage.
implementation: Poll each NMC every 5 minutes via `Splunk_TA_snmp`. Confirm the firmware-specific divisor (`/6000` for hundredths-of-minutes, `/100` on some older NMC2 firmware) with one reference UPS using `snmpwalk -v3 -u <user> <ip> 1.3.6.1.4.1.318.1.1.1.2.2.3`. Alert on `avg_runtime < 15` over `-1h@h`, throttle by `host` for 4h.
visualization: Line chart (`runtime_min` per host over 24h), Single value (worst current UPS by minimum runtime), Table (UPS hosts under threshold sorted by minutes remaining ascending).
knownFalsePositives: Scheduled battery self-test on APC Smart-UPS XL: every 14 days the NMC runs a deep discharge and `upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining` dips for ~5 minutes; suppress by filtering events where `upsBasicBatteryStatus=2` (testing). Generator drills (annual or quarterly) drop the runtime estimate because the firmware re-baselines against the genset frequency; suppress with a `maintenance_windows` lookup keyed on `host` and the drill schedule. Cooling tower load tests on shared distribution circuits cause transient runtime drops; correlate with `index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:pdu` `upsAdvOutputLoad` spikes and suppress when the load spike is < 5 minutes. Battery temperature excursions during summer-peak HVAC events compress the runtime estimate even without load growth; cross-check `iemStatusProbeCurrentTemp` from the BMS modular input before paging.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Replay an APC PowerNet trap stream where `upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining` reports 8 minutes (480000 hundredths) for 6 consecutive 5-minute polls on a single host. The scheduled search executed over `-1h@h` returns one row with `avg_runtime < 15` and the alert action fires the notable + PagerDuty webhook.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Replay a synthetic stream where the same UPS reports 25 minutes (1500000 hundredths) steady-state but with a 90-second dip to 8 minutes during a scheduled self-test (`upsBasicBatteryStatus=2`). The alert MUST NOT fire because the hourly average stays above 15 and the test-window suppression filters the dip; verify zero rows in the 24h alert audit.

evidence: Saved-search `power_ups_runtime_below_15m` (in `Splunk_Power_Monitoring` app) + Glass Table `Data Center Power — UPS Runtime Trend` + the `index=_audit action=alert search_id=*power_ups_runtime*` trail showing alert firings over the audit window.
exclusions: Does not cover non-APC UPS vendors (Eaton, Vertiv, Riello — see UC-15.1.27 family) or in-rack lithium battery cabinets that don't expose PowerNet-MIB. Does not detect imminent battery cell failure before the runtime estimate has collapsed (use UC-15.1.29 `upsAdvBatteryReplaceIndicator` for that pre-warning).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• `Splunk_TA_snmp` (Splunkbase #1537, ≥3.0.0) installed on a heavy forwarder with line-of-sight to the management VLAN.
• Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) deployed if NMC2 firmware is in scope (handles trap forwarding and bulk-walk).
• SNMPv3 credentials in a secret store; ACL allowing the SC4SNMP worker pod or the heavy-forwarder IP to reach UDP/161 on each APC NMC.
• One reference UPS per firmware family available for `snmpwalk` baseline.
• Splunk role `power_ops` with `srchIndexesAllowed=power` for the on-call team.
• License headroom: ~3 KB/event/UPS/poll-interval; at 5-min polls and 200 UPS units that's ~170 MB/day.

Step 1 — Configure SNMP polling
In `Splunk_TA_snmp/local/inputs.conf` on the heavy forwarder:

```ini
[splunk_ta_snmp_input://apc-ups-floor3]
destination = 10.0.42.5
port = 161
version = 3
sec_level = authPriv
sec_name = $SNMP_USER$
auth_protocol = SHA
priv_protocol = AES
object_names = upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining, upsAdvOutputLoad, upsAdvBatteryCapacity, upsAdvBatteryReplaceIndicator, upsBasicBatteryStatus
sourcetype = snmp:apc:ups
index = power
interval = 300
```

Verify via `splunk btool inputs list --debug | grep snmp_input`. Tail `$SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/splunkd.log` for the modular input's `INFO ExecProcessor` lines.

Step 2 — Validate field extraction
`index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host=10.0.42.5 earliest=-1h | head 1 | table _time upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining upsAdvOutputLoad upsAdvBatteryCapacity`. The raw value is hundredths-of-minutes on most NMC2 firmware (divide by 6000 for minutes). Compare against the APC NMC web UI `https://<ups-ip>/admin/UPSStatus.htm` battery-runtime field. Discrepancy > 2 minutes suggests a divisor mismatch — confirm with `snmpwalk -v3 -u <user> <ip> 1.3.6.1.4.1.318.1.1.1.2.2.3` against one reference UPS per firmware family.

Step 3 — Create the scheduled search
```spl
index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host=*
| eval runtime_min=tonumber(upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining)/6000
| timechart span=1h avg(runtime_min) as avg_runtime by host
| where avg_runtime < 15
```

Schedule cron `*/15 * * * *`, time range `-1h@h to @h`. Alert action: notable + PagerDuty events-API webhook. Throttle suppression on the `host` field for 4 hours so the same UPS doesn't re-page during a single incident.

Step 4 — Troubleshoot
• No events arriving — check `splunk btool inputs list --debug` on the heavy forwarder; verify the SNMPv3 ACL on the NMC (`config snmp` over SSH). Check `$SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/splunk_ta_snmp.log` for `ConnectionRefused` or `AuthFailure`.
• Constant `runtime_min` of 1090 — that's the MIB sentinel for 'on bypass' (raw value 65535/6000 ≈ 1092). Add `| where runtime_min < 1090` to the search.
• Runtime spikes during cooling-tower tests — not a UPS issue, it's the shared distribution circuit. Suppress with a `maintenance_windows` lookup keyed on `host`.
• Alert never fires even when dashboard shows < 15 — the alert is using `-15m to now` instead of `-1h@h to @h`. Re-check the saved-search time range.

Step 5 — Operations
Monthly capacity review: query `index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups | stats min(runtime_min) as worst by host | sort worst | head 10`. Add to the `dc_capacity_review` workflow ticket. Annual battery replacement program driven from `upsAdvBatteryReplaceIndicator` (see UC-15.1.29) and from the rolling worst-minutes report. All manual interventions logged through the `power_ops` RBAC role into `index=changes`.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
